heterologous_genes.function: L-lactate dehydrogenase; converts pyruvate + NADH to L-lactate + NAD+

knockouts.rationale: Major pyruvate decarboxylase; blocks ethanol pathway to redirect pyruvate flux toward lactate (WT flux 15.95)
Isozyme of PDC1; must knock out all three PDC genes due to OR-logic GPR to fully disable PYRDC
Third pyruvate decarboxylase isozyme; required for complete PYRDC elimination
Major alcohol dehydrogenase; blocks acetaldehyde to ethanol conversion (WT flux 15.82)
Alcohol dehydrogenase isozyme; OR-logic GPR requires multi-gene knockout
Alcohol dehydrogenase isozyme; completes elimination of ethanol production
Degrades L-lactate back to pyruvate; knockout prevents product degradation
Blocks glycerol pathway to redirect NADH toward LDH reaction (WT flux 0.007)

overexpressions.rationale: Increase pyruvate supply from PEP; ensures high substrate availability for LDH

fba_results.strategy_description: Express heterologous L-LDH from L. casei (P00343). Knock out all three pyruvate decarboxylase genes (PDC1/PDC5/PDC6), three alcohol dehydrogenase genes (ADH1/ADH3/ADH5), CYB2 (lactate degradation), and GPD1 (glycerol pathway). This eliminates ethanol and glycerol as competing pathways, prevents L-lactate degradation, and redirects nearly all glycolytic flux through LDH. With 10% minimum biomass constraint, achieves 19.65 mmol/gDW/h lactate production (98.2% of theoretical maximum 20.0). Production envelope shows linear tradeoff: at full biomass (0.287) production is 15.83; at zero biomass production reaches 20.0.
